name: getHistory
description:
ALWAYS USE THIS TOOL to retrieve historical chat records and past events when a timeframe is known. Fetches directly from the database — never infer historical data from conversation context. If a previous call returned no data, try different start/end parameters or a broader range.
The search automatically covers ALL channels in the current context (primary channel plus all linked channels from channelIds config). Results include a channelId field on each row — group rows by channelId when multiple channels are involved.
TIMEZONE: All timestamps are stored and queried in UTC. The runtime info block contains current_time_iso (UTC) and timezone_hint (user's local timezone). Always convert local times to UTC before passing start/end — for example, if the user is in CEST (UTC+2) and a session ran 20:00–22:00 CEST, pass start='HH:MM:SS in UTC' (18:00–20:00 UTC). Never pass local-time values directly.
requirements:
- for targeted retrieval: a known timeframe (start/end timestamps in UTC); if unknown, use a timeline overview tool first to identify relevant periods

start.description: REQUIRED: start timestamp in UTC (YYYY-MM-DD, DD.MM.YYYY, or ISO 8601 with timezone offset). If date-only, time defaults to 00:00:00 UTC. Convert local times to UTC before passing — e.g. 20:00 CEST = 18:00 UTC.

end.description: OPTIONAL: end timestamp in UTC. If omitted, retrieval is open-ended from start until now (UTC). If date-only, end is exclusive at next day 00:00:00 UTC.

prompt.description: OPTIONAL: additional instructions to steer the AI summary (e.g. 'focus on combat events').

startCtxId.description: OPTIONAL: continue pagination from after this ctx_id (applies across all channels). Legacy aliases: start_ctx_id, start_ctx.
